steps.title: Understand what a point on the graph means
steps.content: If a point $(x, y)$ lies on the graph of $y = f(x)$, then by definition, the $y$-value at that point equals $f$ evaluated at the $x$-value. In other words, $f(x) = y$.

steps.title: Apply the definition to the given point
steps.content: The graph passes through $(5, 3)$. The $x$-coordinate is 5 and the $y$-coordinate is 3. Therefore, $f(5) = 3$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
